mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier financial conditions, lower discount rates, and higher equity multiples; when the policy rate is in a downtrend, S&P 500 forward returns over the next month are positive because valuation expansion dominates modest growth concerns.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods after a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal spending and revenue growth, which lifts near-term earnings expectations and supports equity prices; the effect is strongest when retail sales are accelerating versus their recent 3-month average.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change turns positive after being negative
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce non-positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of cases, or if the average forward return after positive RSAFS acceleration is not above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS signals labor-market tightness and income growth, which supports consumption and reduces recession risk; equity returns over the next 60 trading days should be positive when unemployment is falling while payrolls are still expanding.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by at least 0.2pp over 3 months while PAYEMS 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either proxy alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than half of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rising INDPRO index signals improving industrial output and cyclical activity, which tends to strengthen earnings expectations for broad equities; the signal should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when industrial production is above its recent trend.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month moving average rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the crossover does not yield a positive average 20-day forward return, or if its hit rate is not materially above 50% over a rolling 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST captures a sharp housing demand slowdown and rising recession risk, which should pressure cyclical earnings and equity valuations; the next 20 trading days should be negative when housing starts fall sharply from their recent baseline.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if sharp HOUST declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is rising while FEDFUNDS remains near the lower bound, real rates stay suppressed and financial conditions remain accommodative, which should support equity multiples and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 1 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-minus-policy gap does not coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of triggered cases, or if the spread has no incremental predictive value beyond either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE is a direct recession warning that weakens consumption and corporate profit expectations, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days after unemployment has risen materially from its recent low.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp from its 6-month low
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative on average after the trigger, or if the trigger fails to beat the unconditional negative-return rate by at least 10 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates synchronized real output and nominal demand expansion, which should lift earnings expectations and support equities; the effect should be strongest over a quarterly horizon when both series are above their recent trend.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDPC1-RSAFS strength does not improve 60-day forward returns relative to each series alone, or if the combined signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.03.
